questions: A person with a pre-existing hypertension diagnosis (disclosed at inception) is 30 months into their policy. They are hospitalized for a hypertensive emergency. Is it covered?

answers.answer: The policy covers the treatment of hypertensive emergency as it is a direct complication of the pre-existing hypertension diagnosis, and the 36-month waiting period has not expired yet.

relevant_clauses.section: SECTION D) EXCLUSIONS- STANDARD EXCLUSIONS APPLICABLE TO PART A- DOMESTIC COVER
relevant_clauses.content: Expenses related to the treatment of a Pre-Existing Disease (PED) and its direct complications shall be excluded until the expiry of 36 months of continuous coverage after the date of inception of the first Global Health Care Policy with Us.

relevant_clauses.section: SECTION D) EXCLUSIONS- STANDARD EXCLUSIONS APPLICABLE TO PART B- INTERNATIONAL COVER
relevant_clauses.content: Expenses related to the treatment of a Pre-Existing Disease (PED) and its direct complications shall be excluded until the expiry of 36 months of continuous coverage after the date of inception of the first Global Health Care Policy with Insurer.
